skill.name: devops-deployment
skill.description: Plan CI/CD pipelines, containers, Kubernetes manifests, GitOps workflows, and infrastructure templates for deployment projects.
skill.summary: DevOps deployment guidance with reusable CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, ArgoCD, Helm, and secrets templates.
skill.author: ArieGoldkin

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, secret, and weak-crypto patterns, including one critical combined-behavior heuristic. Review found no prompt injection, malicious exfiltration, or hidden executable code; most high-severity scanner hits are deployment-template placeholders or false positives. The skill should publish with a medium-risk warning because copyable templates include default development credentials, mutable image references, and production-facing network scaffolding.

medium_findings.title: Copyable Default Database Credentials
medium_findings.description: The Docker Compose template includes a development DATABASE_URL with postgres:postgres and a POSTGRES_PASSWORD value of postgres. This is a true positive for sensitive configuration risk if users copy the template into shared or production environments, although the file labels it as development-only.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credentials are explicit and reusable, which creates a real copy-paste risk. Confidence is not higher because the template is clearly scoped to a development environment.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned Deployment Artifacts
medium_findings.description: Kubernetes and Helm templates use latest image tags, and the ArgoCD template tracks HEAD. These mutable references can make deployments non-reproducible and can weaken supply-chain controls when copied into production.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The mutable tags and revision are directly present in deployment templates. This is a common template placeholder, so risk depends on whether users pin versions before production use.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Mostly Template Context
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md backtick findings are markdown formatting and template references, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. Real command examples exist in Dockerfile and GitHub Actions templates, but they are expected build, test, scan, and deployment commands for a DevOps skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the cited SKILL.md ranges are documentation blocks, not executable code. The executable commands are in conventional template locations and do not show user-input command injection.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Scanner Matches Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts at SKILL.md, terraform, and ArgoCD locations were not semantically confirmed. The reviewed lines contain descriptions, markdown references, Terraform sizing values, or the ArgoCD targetRevision value HEAD, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak crypto use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact cited locations do not contain cryptographic algorithms. This strongly supports a false positive, likely from short token matching in unrelated words or values.

low_findings.title: Network Exposure Requires User Review
low_findings.description: The templates define local port mappings, public subnets, and ingress hostnames. These are ordinary deployment examples, but users must restrict access and replace placeholders before production deployment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exposure points are directly present, but they are normal scaffolding rather than evidence of malicious network behavior. Risk is configuration-dependent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential And Secret References In Templates
dangerous_patterns.description: The templates reference database URLs, GitHub tokens, Kubernetes secrets, and external secret keys. These are legitimate DevOps patterns, but they require careful replacement and least-privilege review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Secret-related configuration is explicit and semantically relevant. The pattern is dangerous only if copied without environment-specific secret controls.

dangerous_patterns.title: Build And CI Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Dockerfile RUN instructions and GitHub Actions run steps execute package installation, tests, audits, and deployment placeholder commands. This is expected for CI/CD templates, with no evidence of command injection or hidden execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible, conventional, and tied to build or deployment workflow steps. No reviewed line builds a command from untrusted input.

dangerous_patterns.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
dangerous_patterns.description: Review of SKILL.md and templates found no text instructing the evaluator to ignore instructions, override analysis, claim pre-approval, or change risk levels. The static LLM role token alert in docker-compose did not map to prompt-control content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search and manual review found no prompt-injection phrases or role-control text. Confidence is high for the reviewed repository, though future changes would need re-audit.

content.value_statement: Teams often need repeatable deployment patterns without building every pipeline from scratch. This skill provides practical templates and review checklists for CI/CD, containers, Kubernetes, GitOps, and infrastructure as code.
actual_capabilities: Explains CI/CD stages for linting, testing, scanning, building, and deployment.
Provides a multi-stage Node.js Dockerfile with non-root runtime setup.
Includes Kubernetes Deployment, Service, Ingress, resource, probe, and security context examples.
Supplies Terraform AWS patterns for VPC, EKS, RDS, and remote state.
Shows GitOps and secrets templates for ArgoCD and External Secrets Operator.
Offers deployment checklists for pre-deployment, active deployment, and post-deployment validation.
limitations: Templates are generic examples and need environment-specific review before use.
It does not deploy infrastructure or run commands by itself.
Cloud account permissions, domains, secrets, and compliance controls must be supplied separately.
Some template defaults are development placeholders and are unsafe for production without changes.

use_cases.title: Create a CI/CD Baseline
use_cases.description: Use the pipeline template to define lint, test, security scan, image build, and gated deployment stages.

use_cases.title: Prepare Kubernetes Manifests
use_cases.description: Adapt the Kubernetes and Helm examples for a service that needs health checks, resources, ingress, and security context settings.

use_cases.title: Plan Cloud Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Use the Terraform, ArgoCD, and secrets examples to outline AWS infrastructure and GitOps delivery workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Deployment Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a simple release readiness review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-deployment skill to create a release checklist for my Node.js service. Include CI checks, deployment verification, rollback planning, and post-release monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Containerize an Application
prompt_templates.scenario: You need Docker guidance for an existing service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-deployment skill to adapt the Dockerfile pattern for my application. Explain the build stages, runtime user, health check, and production hardening changes I should make.

prompt_templates.title: Design Kubernetes Delivery
prompt_templates.scenario: You need manifests and operational settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-deployment skill to design Kubernetes deployment guidance for my service. Cover Deployment, Service, Ingress, resource limits, probes, autoscaling, secrets, and rollout strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Production Deployment Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: You need an advanced architecture and risk review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-deployment skill to review my production deployment plan across GitHub Actions, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, ArgoCD, and External Secrets. Identify risks, missing gates, and concrete hardening steps.

output_examples.input: Plan a first CI/CD pipeline for a web service.
output: A staged pipeline outline covering linting, tests, security scanning, image build, registry publishing, staging deployment, and production approval.
A short list of required repository secrets, permissions, and environment gates.
A release checklist for monitoring health checks, logs, and rollback readiness.
A readiness assessment covering probes, resource requests, non-root runtime settings, ingress TLS, and secret references.
Recommended changes for pinned images, production hostnames, least-privilege service accounts, and autoscaling targets.

output_examples.input: Prepare an AWS infrastructure plan for a service.
output: A high-level plan for VPC, private subnets, EKS nodes, RDS, remote Terraform state, and deployment ownership.
A risk list covering public exposure, secrets handling, backup retention, state locking, and environment separation.

best_practices: Replace every placeholder domain, repository, image tag, secret name, and credential before using a template.
Pin container images, Git revisions, action versions, provider versions, and deployment artifacts for production.
Review generated deployment plans with security, operations, and application owners before applying them.
anti_patterns: Do not reuse the development database password or connection string outside local development.
Do not deploy latest tags or HEAD revisions to production without a controlled release process.
Do not expose ports, ingress hosts, or public subnets without access controls and monitoring.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy applications automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and templates. Users must review, adapt, and run deployment commands in their own environment.

faq.question: Can I use the templates in production?
faq.answer: Yes, after replacing placeholders, pinning versions, securing secrets, and reviewing infrastructure settings for your organization.

faq.question: Which deployment tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers GitHub Actions, Docker, Docker Compose, Kubernetes, Helm values, Terraform on AWS, ArgoCD, and External Secrets Operator.

faq.question: Does it include secrets management guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes External Secrets Operator examples and Kubernetes secret references, but users must provide secure secret stores and access policies.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains deployment templates with commands, network exposure, and secret references. These are legitimate but risky if copied without review.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, with prompts suited to deployment planning and review.
